codeReview.summary: The fix introduces LogEmitter-based interception and improves several startup/sync checks, but VAL-CROSS-006 is still not fully satisfied because at least one startup/sync assertion path can pass without any emitted production log capture and still depends on test-local helper collection.

issues.description: In `test_startupProfileFetch_capturesRuntimeLogs_noSecrets`, the test clears `capturedLogMessages` and then calls `fetchAllProfiles()` (non-logging path) before asserting on `allLogs` merged with `RuntimeLogCapture` helper strings. Because there is no emitted-log assertion (e.g., non-empty captured production logs), this startup check can pass with zero intercepted production log output, which does not meet the emitted-log evidence expectation for VAL-CROSS-006.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker set `followedProcedure=true`, but handoff verification omits required context-read evidence and full procedure coverage described by the skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md requires explicit context-read evidence in `verification.commandsRun` when `followedProcedure=true` and lists a broader validation stack. The handoff for session a129cc65-a840-4179-b844-8d3fbb01bfc7 only reports one scoped xcodebuild command.

summary: Compared with the original failed feature/review, this rerun materially improves runtime interception by introducing `LogEmitter` and wiring startup/sync tests to capture emitted messages in multiple cases. However, a startup fetch assertion still permits helper-only evidence without guaranteed emitted-log capture, so VAL-CROSS-006 is not yet fully proven.
